methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pyka is a Series B autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The sector peer universe comprises 5 same-sector private companies including Joby Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Archer Aviation, though direct valuation comparables at Pyka's stage remain sparse. No public drones company peers exist with sufficient overlap to support meaningful benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given insufficient narrow peer density. Using Pyka's $40M total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement yields $76M. This reflects the expected valuation increase required to justify Series B investor returns relative to prior rounds. The peer-median method could not be applied; while 5 same-sector peers exist, filtered subsets meeting stage, timing, and valuation-tier criteria fell below the three-datapoint minimum threshold necessary for reliable median calculation. Drawing on all-sector median would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of business models and geographies within the broader Drones & Autonomous landscape.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $65M to $87M, with $76M as the median point. This range reflects one-method triangulation and carries material uncertainty around Series B step-up dynamics specific to autonomous systems, the lack of matched peer transactions, and nascent market conditions in commercial drone deployment. Key variables for resolution include customer traction, regulatory clearances, and comparable Series B pricing in adjacent autonomous vehicle subsectors.
